payload.source_text: High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.name: Participant Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for defining and maintaining the roles, relationships, and responsibilities of family members who participate in the household economic system, establishing clear boundaries of authority and entitlement.

payload.name: Work-Reward Obligation Framework
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to establishing and governing the relationship between defined work units and their corresponding monetary entitlements, including the rules for defining, claiming, and fulfilling these obligations.

payload.name: Financial Disbursement Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise responsibility for accurate calculation, transparent reporting, and timely transfer of earned monetary entitlements according to established performance records and periodic settlement cycles.

payload.name: Transparency and Information Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise obligation to provide participants with visibility into available opportunities, current status of commitments, and financial positions to enable informed participation and decision-making within the system.

payload.name: Historical Record Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise duty to maintain persistent, auditable records of all completed obligations and financial transactions, ensuring long-term accountability, dispute resolution capability, and institutional memory.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, governing, and maintaining the lifecycle of work obligations that children may undertake for compensation, including establishment of value, completion criteria, and recurring availability patterns.

payload.name: Child Participation Entitlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for enabling children to engage with available work obligations, claim those they will perform, execute them, and establish their entitlement to compensation through demonstrated completion.

payload.name: Earnings Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for determining, accumulating, and reporting the monetary obligations owed to participants based on their completed work within defined temporal boundaries, enabling settlement fulfillment.

payload.name: Parental Oversight
payload.description: Business responsibility for providing visibility into child participation patterns, completion achievements, and fulfillment obligations to enable informed parental governance and payment execution.

payload.name: Historical Record Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for preserving evidence of past task definitions, participation events, and completion outcomes across temporal boundaries to support accountability, pattern analysis, and governance continuity.

payload.name: Temporal Aggregation Logic
payload.description: Logical derivation behavior that aggregates earnings data within weekly temporal boundaries. Defines how earnings values are logically accumulated and bounded by time cycles without prescribing calculation algorithms or reporting mechanisms.

payload.name: Task Lifecycle State Model
payload.description: Logical state transitions governing task availability and the temporal retention policy for historical task records. Defines how tasks move through availability states and when historical data is logically preserved, triggered by weekly cycle boundaries.

payload.name: Task Completion Entity Model
payload.description: Logical structure defining the core completion entity and its associated child entities, including the identity, attributes, and containment relationships that represent a task completion occurrence within the system's logical boundary.

payload.name: Completion History Association Model
payload.description: Logical relationship model governing how completion occurrences are linked to their historical counterparts and task references, defining the traceability structure between active and archived completion records.

payload.name: OS Platform Compatibility
payload.description: Physical platform constraints defining which operating system versions the system must be built to support, establishing baseline compatibility requirements for deployment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise children as participants in the household economic system.
payload.rationale: Establishes the Child entity as a structural participant within the enterprise scope, providing the foundation for role-based obligations.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise parents as participants in the household economic system.
payload.rationale: Establishes the Parent entity as a structural participant within the enterprise scope, providing the foundation for role-based obligations.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim tasks.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to provide the Child role with task claiming capability, derived from the role definition as task claimant.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to view earnings.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to provide the Child role with earnings viewing capability, derived from the role definition as earnings viewer.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall facilitate disbursement of rewards.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to facilitate disbursement, derived from the role definition of Parent as disbursement facilitator.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish tasks as work units.
payload.rationale: Entity definition at enterprise scope — task is the work unit the enterprise recognises.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish monetary reward amounts as attributes of tasks.
payload.rationale: Structural relationship between task entity and its monetary attribute at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain task completion status.
payload.rationale: Task completion status is a strategic state attribute the enterprise must track.

payload.rationale: How-column process commitment — enterprise enables parent capability to define reward units.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a weekly settlement cycle for earnings accumulation.
payload.rationale: Defines the temporal governance constraint for the earnings accumulation cycle at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide for pocket money disbursement by parents based on reported earnings.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into available tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to enable informed participation by ensuring children can see task opportunities. Derived from the visibility provision function and the goal of task awareness.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into claimed tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to enable children to track their claimed commitments. Derived from the visibility provision function and the goal of task awareness.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into their earnings.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to support financial literacy by enabling children to view their accumulated earnings and understand the financial outcomes of completed tasks.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain persistent records of all completed obligations.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise's commitment to maintain historical records of completed obligations. All three CCIs support this obligation: the What-column identifies the historical record artifact, the How-column addresses the maintenance function, and the Why-column establishes the accountability purpose.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain persistent records of all financial transactions.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise's commitment to maintain historical records of financial transactions. All three CCIs support this obligation: the What-column identifies the historical record artifact, the How-column addresses the maintenance function, and the Why-column establishes the accountability purpose.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall ensure historical records are auditable.
payload.rationale: Constrains the quality of maintained records to be auditable, which is a governance rule on how records must be kept. Both CCIs contribute to this auditability constraint: the What-column characterizes the record as an accountability artifact and the Why-column establishes accountability as the governing principle.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical records to support dispute resolution.
payload.rationale: Constrains the purpose for which historical records must be maintained—dispute resolution support. This is a governance rule expressing the Why obligation: accountability for resolving disputes requires records to be kept.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical records to support institutional memory.
payload.rationale: Constrains the purpose for which historical records must be maintained—institutional memory support. This is a governance rule expressing the Why obligation: accountability for preserving institutional knowledge requires records to be kept.

payload.statement: The earnings aggregation service shall use ISO week start and end timestamps as query boundaries.
payload.rationale: Physical query construction obligation specifying temporal boundary logic for weekly period definition.

payload.statement: The system shall persist task completion records in a relational data store.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of historical task record maintenance requires selection of persistence technology—relational store chosen for structured historical data.

payload.statement: The task completion record schema shall include foreign key associations to child entity and weekly period identifiers.
payload.rationale: Physical schema structure realising the logical association between historical records, children, and weekly periods via relational foreign keys.

payload.statement: The mobile application shall provide user interface components displaying aggregated earnings values to authenticated child users.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of earnings visibility to child entities via mobile application UI components for the entitled user role.

payload.statement: The mobile application shall provide user interface components displaying derived earnings values to authenticated parent users.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of earnings visibility to parent entities via mobile application UI components for disbursement decision support.

payload.statement: The mobile application shall provide user interface components displaying tasks in available state to authenticated child users.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of task visibility filtering via mobile application UI components restricted to available-state tasks for child role.

payload.statement: The mobile application shall provide user interface components displaying claimed tasks exclusively to the child user associated with each claim.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of role-based task visibility via mobile application UI components filtering claimed tasks by associated child entity.

payload.statement: The mobile application shall provide user interface components displaying earnings values to authenticated child users entitled to those earnings.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of earnings visibility to entitled child entities via mobile application UI components with entitlement filtering.

payload.statement: The task instance schema shall include a completion state attribute with defined enumerated values.
payload.rationale: Physical schema structure realising the logical association between task instances and completion states via a structured attribute in the relational schema.

payload.statement: The system shall persist earnings derivation records in a relational data store.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of earnings derivation record maintenance via relational persistence technology for structured financial data.

payload.statement: The system shall retain task completion records in persistent storage beyond the active task lifecycle termination.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of historical record retention via persistence layer configuration ensuring records survive active lifecycle deletion.

payload.statement: The relational data store shall enforce immutability constraints on historical task completion record tables.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of logical immutability via database-level constraints (e.g., triggers, permissions) preventing modification of historical records.
payload.fit_criteria: Historical task records shall reject all UPDATE operations after initial INSERT.

payload.statement: The historical task record schema shall include a foreign key reference to the corresponding active task completion entity identifier.
payload.rationale: Physical schema structure realising traceability between active and historical task entities via relational foreign key association.

payload.statement: The system shall create persistent historical task records for all task instances transitioning to completed state.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of universal historical record retention via persistence layer logic triggered by task completion lifecycle events.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce that task availability state transitions occur only at weekly period boundaries defined by ISO 8601 week numbering.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of weekly temporal boundaries via application-layer enforcement logic using standardised week definition (ISO 8601) for state transition timing.
payload.fit_criteria: Task state transitions from unavailable to available shall occur exclusively at 00:00:00 on the first day of each ISO 8601 week.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce that earnings aggregation calculations execute only at weekly period boundaries defined by ISO 8601 week numbering.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of weekly temporal boundaries for earnings calculations via scheduled execution logic aligned to ISO 8601 week transitions.
payload.fit_criteria: Earnings aggregation processes shall initiate exclusively at 00:00:00 on the first day of each ISO 8601 week.

payload.statement: The system shall persist child records as a distinct entity type in the physical data store.
payload.rationale: Realises the logical child entity as a physical persistent entity in the data store.

payload.statement: The system shall implement a physical foreign-key relationship between task completion records and child records.
payload.rationale: Implements the logical association as a physical database relationship linking completion events to children.

payload.statement: The system shall implement a calculation service component that computes weekly earnings totals from task completion records grouped by child and week.
payload.rationale: Realises the earnings derivation logic as a physical service component performing aggregation.

payload.statement: The system shall persist a weekly earnings amount field in the child record schema.
payload.rationale: Implements the logical association as a physical attribute in the child entity schema.

payload.statement: The calculation service shall enforce weekly temporal boundaries using ISO 8601 week numbering when aggregating earnings.
payload.rationale: Establishes physical temporal boundary enforcement within the calculation component.
payload.fit_criteria: Aggregation queries filter on ISO week number and year

payload.statement: The calculation service shall execute an aggregation query that sums completion values partitioned by child identifier and ISO week boundary.
payload.rationale: Specifies the physical aggregation operation within the calculation service.

payload.statement: The parent-facing interface component shall expose earnings data retrieved from the child earnings schema.
payload.rationale: Realises logical visibility as a physical interface component serving earnings data to parents.

payload.statement: The parent-facing interface component shall enforce authorization rules that restrict earnings data access to children associated with the requesting parent identifier.
payload.rationale: Implements logical governance constraint as physical authorization enforcement in the interface layer.
payload.fit_criteria: Access control checks verify parent-child association before returning earnings data

payload.statement: The system shall expose earnings amounts via an API endpoint accessible to payment processing components.
payload.rationale: Realises payment fulfillment availability as a physical API integration point.

payload.statement: The system shall persist parent records as a distinct entity type with a one-to-many relationship schema to child records.
payload.rationale: Implements the logical parent entity and governance association as physical schema structures.

payload.statement: The parent-facing interface component shall expose task claim event records filtered by associated child identifiers.
payload.rationale: Realises logical claim event visibility as a physical interface query capability.

payload.statement: The parent-facing interface component shall expose task completion records filtered by associated child identifiers.
payload.rationale: Realises logical completion visibility as a physical interface query capability.

payload.statement: The system shall persist historical task records in a time-series data structure associated with task definition identifiers.
payload.rationale: Implements logical historical record entity as a physical time-series schema structure.

payload.statement: The data store shall enforce retention policies that archive or purge historical task records based on configurable temporal thresholds.
payload.rationale: Realises logical retention policy as physical data lifecycle enforcement in the persistence layer.
payload.fit_criteria: Records older than retention threshold are archived or purged within 24 hours

payload.statement: The system shall implement a physical foreign-key relationship between task completion event records and child records.
payload.rationale: Implements the logical association as a physical database relationship linking completion events to children.

payload.statement: The system shall persist task completion event records in a relational database with temporal retention metadata.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of retention policy compliance requires database persistence with temporal metadata to enable policy enforcement.

payload.statement: The system shall implement automated purging of historical task data based on configurable retention period thresholds.
payload.rationale: Physical mechanism to enforce retention duration requires automated purging infrastructure at the data storage layer.

payload.statement: The system shall update task state records in the persistent store when a child user submits a claim transaction.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of state transition requires database write operations triggered by user transaction.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce state precondition validation at the application service layer before processing claim transactions.
payload.rationale: Physical enforcement of state constraint requires validation logic in the service component that processes claim requests.

payload.statement: The system shall execute scheduled batch processes to transition task states at weekly intervals.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of weekly cycle transitions requires a scheduled job infrastructure component.

payload.statement: The parent user interface component shall retrieve aggregated completion event data from the application service layer.
payload.rationale: Physical integration obligation: the UI component must obtain aggregated completion event data via backend service integration.

payload.statement: The parent user interface component shall display aggregated completion event data.
payload.rationale: Physical presentation obligation: the UI component must render the aggregated completion event data to the user.

payload.statement: The child user interface component shall query available-state tasks via REST API.
payload.rationale: Physical API integration obligation to retrieve filtered task data from backend service.

payload.statement: The child user interface component shall display available-state tasks.
payload.rationale: Physical UI rendering obligation to present task visibility to the user.

payload.statement: The child user interface component shall query completed-state tasks via REST API.
payload.rationale: Physical integration obligation: UI component must retrieve completed tasks through REST API endpoint with status filtering.

payload.statement: The child user interface component shall display completed-state tasks.
payload.rationale: Physical presentation obligation: UI component must render completed tasks retrieved from backend.

payload.statement: The system shall persist task completion event records with timestamp when initiated by child users.
payload.rationale: Physical capture of completion events requires database write operations storing event timestamp attribute.

payload.statement: The system shall persist task completion event records with user identifier when initiated by child users.
payload.rationale: Physical capture of completion events requires database write operations storing user identifier attribute.

payload.statement: The task database table shall include a monetary value column of numeric data type.
payload.rationale: Physical schema realisation of the monetary value attribute requires a database column definition.

payload.statement: The task database table shall include a completion status column with enumerated state values.
payload.rationale: Physical schema realisation of status attribute requires a database column with constrained value set.

payload.statement: The parent user interface component shall provide task creation forms integrated with the task management service API.
payload.rationale: Physical provision of task definition capability requires UI component with API integration for task creation.

payload.statement: The task database table shall include a foreign key column referencing the parent user table.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of entity association requires relational database foreign key constraint.

payload.statement: The system shall execute scheduled batch processes to update task availability status fields at weekly intervals.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of weekly state transitions requires scheduled job infrastructure updating database records.

payload.statement: The system shall implement soft-delete or archival mechanisms for historical task records in the database.
payload.rationale: Physical preservation of historical records requires database-level archival or soft-delete infrastructure.

payload.statement: The system shall persist task completion event records in a relational data store.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of the logical task completion event entity requires a concrete persistence mechanism; relational storage provides structured record-keeping.

payload.statement: The task completion event record schema shall include a foreign key relationship to the child record schema.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of the logical association between task completion events and child entities requires a concrete relational schema constraint.

payload.statement: The system shall persist historical task records in a relational data store.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of the logical historical task records entity requires a concrete persistence mechanism; relational storage provides structured archival.

payload.statement: The system shall write each task completion event occurrence as a distinct record with a unique primary key.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of recording distinct logical instances requires concrete database write operations with unique identifiers per occurrence.

payload.statement: The task definition schema shall be accessible via API to parent application components.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of logical accessibility to parent entities requires a concrete interface mechanism; API exposure provides component-level access.

payload.statement: The system shall implement a scheduled service that updates task availability state fields at weekly cycle boundaries.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of logical state transitions at weekly boundaries requires a concrete scheduling component and database update mechanism.

payload.statement: The system shall copy task records to the historical record schema before updating availability state fields.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of preserving historical records during state transitions requires a concrete copy operation between schema structures.

payload.statement: The system shall support iOS 14.0 or later.
payload.rationale: Establishes the baseline iOS operating system version compatibility constraint that governs the physical deployment target for the mobile application on Apple devices.
payload.fit_criteria: Successful installation and execution on devices running iOS 14.0, iOS 15.x, and iOS 16.x.

payload.statement: The system shall support Android 10 (API level 29) or later.
payload.rationale: Establishes the baseline Android operating system version compatibility constraint that governs the physical deployment target for the mobile application on Android devices.
payload.fit_criteria: Successful installation and execution on devices running Android 10, Android 11, Android 12, and Android 13.
